items.title: Always `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)`
items.why: Cascading config (level, handlers) works automatically; you can flip one subsystem to DEBUG without flooding everything.
items.example: `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)` at the top of every module.

items.title: Use lazy formatting in log calls
items.why: Lazy formatting skips work entirely when the level is suppressed; matters on hot paths.
items.example: Avoid f-strings in `logger.debug(...)` calls that fire millions of times.

items.title: Configure once, in the application
items.prefer: One `dictConfig(...)` call at app startup; libraries only call `getLogger`.
items.why: Centralised config = predictable formatting and destinations; library-side config fights the app's intent.

items.title: Structured (JSON) logs + correlation IDs for anything multi-process
items.avoid: Plain text logs interleaving across pods.
items.why: Without correlation IDs, log lines from concurrent requests are unreadable noise.

items.title: Redact sensitive data in logs
items.avoid: Logging full prompts containing PII or raw API keys.
items.prefer: A `Filter` or formatter step that redacts known patterns.
items.why: Logs travel — to vendor stores, backups, copies in tickets. Each accidental leak is a real incident.
